# Glenn Hanns ACS - Cinematographer & Director of Photography
> Glenn Hanns ACS is an award-winning Australian cinematographer and Director of Photography based in Sydney, NSW. He is an accredited member of the Australian Cinematographers Society (ACS) and holds a Masters from the Australian Film Television and Radio School (AFTRS) with High Achievement in Cinematography. His work spans feature films, television drama, dramatised documentary, short film, music video and commercials, with international festival screenings and distribution on Netflix, Amazon Prime, Lionsgate, Apple TV, Foxtel, Binge, ABC, SBS and Discovery Channel.

## Pages
- [Homepage](https://glennhanns.com/): About Glenn Hanns ACS — biography, career credits, awards, festival screenings and industry recognition
- [Work](https://glennhanns.com/work.html): Cinematography portfolio — showreel, project breakdowns and production credits
- [Journal](https://glennhanns.com/blog.html): Writing on cinematography, visual storytelling and the craft of the Director of Photography
- [What a Feature Film Cinematographer Does](https://glennhanns.com/blog/what-does-a-feature-film-cinematographer-do.html): The role of a DOP across pre-production, principal photography and post
- [How to Hire a Cinematographer for Feature Film](https://glennhanns.com/blog/how-to-hire-a-cinematographer-for-feature-film.html): What producers and directors should look for when hiring a DOP
- [What a Television Series Cinematographer Does](https://glennhanns.com/blog/what-a-television-series-cinematographer-does.html): How a TV series DP builds a repeatable visual system across episodic production
- [TV Drama Series Director of Photography](https://glennhanns.com/blog/tv-drama-series-director-of-photography.html): Visual authorship, colour pipeline and performance-driven cinematography in drama series
- [What a Short Film Cinematographer Does](https://glennhanns.com/blog/what-a-short-film-cinematographer-does-and-why-it-matters.html): The specific demands of short-form narrative cinematography
- [What Award-Winning Cinematographers Bring](https://glennhanns.com/blog/what-award-winning-cinematographers-bring.html): How industry recognition reflects professional judgement and craft
- [The Strategic Value of Director of Photography Services](https://glennhanns.com/blog/the-strategic-value-of-director-of-photography-services.html): Why experienced DOP services matter to producers and directors
- [The Editor in the Director's Chair — Josh Groom](https://glennhanns.com/blog/editor-in-the-directors-chair-josh-groom.html): Creative collaboration with editor-turned-director Josh Groom
- [Working with Directors](https://glennhanns.com/blog/working-with-directors.html): Long-term creative partnerships with Kurt Martin, John Mavety and Josh Groom
- [Moon Rock For Monday — Cinematography](https://glennhanns.com/blog/moon-rock-for-monday-cinematography.html): Camera and lighting breakdown for the award-winning feature
- [Forbidden Ground — Cinematography](https://glennhanns.com/blog/forbidden-ground-cinematography.html): WWI feature cinematography breakdown including pyrotechnics and dual-camera workflow
- [Cinematographer vs Camera Operator](https://glennhanns.com/blog/cinematographer-vs-camera-operator.html): The distinction between visual authorship and physical frame execution on a professional set
- [How to Brief a DOP](https://glennhanns.com/blog/how-to-brief-a-dop.html): What makes a great creative brief — and why the best partnerships start with a conversation, not a shot list

## Awards
- 2024 National Award of Distinction — ACS (Australian Cinematography Society) (Moon Rock For Monday)
- 2023 Silver Logie — Most Outstanding Factual Program (Ron Iddles: The Good Cop)
- 2023 Gold ACS (Australian Cinematography Society) NSW & ACT — Dramatised Documentaries (Ron Iddles: The Good Cop)
- 2020 Gold ACS (Australian Cinematography Society) — Feature Films under 2M (Moon Rock For Monday)
- 2020 AACTA Nomination — Best Cinematography (Moon Rock For Monday)
- 2020 SCHLiNGEL SLM Top Award — Best International Feature Film (Moon Rock For Monday)
- 2020 ASIN Award — Best Feature Film (Moon Rock For Monday) — 8 wins & 28 nominations total
- 2013 Walkley Award — Outstanding Journalism (Persons of Interest)
- 2013 MIFF Official Selection (Persons of Interest)
- 2013 Bronze ACS (Australian Cinematography Society) NSW & ACT (Forbidden Ground)
- 2008 Golden Tadpole Nominee — Camerimage Poland, Student Etudes (Bleeders)
- 2008 Official Selection — Festival de Cannes des Antipodes (Bleeders)
- 2008 Official Selection — San Sebastian Film Festival (Bleeders)
- 2008 Official Selection — Camerimage Student Etudes (Bleeders)
